## **Heterogeneity**: Style and Prioritization {.nav-feedback}

- For the same reward $R$, the existence of a planner $p$ can result in heterogenous feedback. [@occam]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"} <!-- 1. User styles 2. Reasoning capabilities -->
- People prioritize various objectives to different degrees. [@mobo, @imitationbook]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

<hr>

<div style="margin-top: -0.5em;">
MIND-MELD learns operator styles by learning personalized embeddings and correction terms given calibration tasks [@mindmeld]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}

Several approaches fail here.

- Example: COACH cannot learn from heterogeneous 'coaches'. [@coach]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}
<!-- 1. Users may desire different *tradeoffs* of objectives 2. Different individuals may prioritize different goals (safety versus speed) -->
</div>
